name: chart/bar-chart
description: Bar chart with vertical/horizontal orientation, grouped or stacked modes, and multi-series support.

meta.name: BarChart
meta.description: Bar chart with vertical/horizontal orientation, grouped or stacked modes, and multi-series support.

props.name: series
props.description: Series array. Each series accepts an optional `color` (any CSS color) overriding the positional palette for that series/stacked layer.

props.name: x
props.description: Accessor returning the category label for a datum.

props.name: y
props.description: Accessor returning the numeric value for a datum.

props.name: title
props.description: Title text for the component.

props.name: description
props.description: Supporting description text.

props.name: orientation
props.description: Layout orientation of the component.

props.name: mode
props.description: 'percent' stacks each category and normalizes it to 100%.

props.name: width
props.description: Width of the component.

props.name: height
props.description: Height of the component.

props.name: xTicks
props.description: Approximate number of ticks on the x-axis.

props.name: yTicks
props.description: Approximate number of ticks on the y-axis.

props.name: xLabelEvery
props.description: Show every Nth category label (always the last) to thin a crowded x-axis.

props.name: legend
props.description: Whether to show the legend.

props.name: tooltip
props.description: Whether to show tooltips on hover.

props.name: tooltipFormat
props.description: Custom tooltip formatter. The stacked default lists "label · total" + each non-zero layer in its color.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class names merged onto the root element.

props.name: plain
props.description: Marks only — no axes, grid lines, or legend. For micro/inline charts.

props.name: annotations
props.description: Reference lines, shaded bands, and markers drawn over the plot (e.g. a target/threshold line).

props.name: labels
props.type: boolean | { format?: (v: number) => string; position?: string }
props.description: Print each value as a label on the mark (collision-aware, decorative/aria-hidden).

props.name: onSelect
props.description: Fired when a point is clicked or activated (Enter/Space) — for drill-down.

props.name: fill
props.description: Bar fill style — solid, a gradient, or a pattern.

props.name: patternKind
props.description: Pattern motif when fill="pattern".

typeDefs.name: BarChartSeries<Datum>
typeDefs.description: One series (a set of bars). Pass an array via the `series` prop.

fields.name: data
fields.description: Row data read by the `x`/`y` accessors.

fields.name: color
fields.description: Any CSS color overriding the positional palette (--cascivo-chart-N) for this series / stacked layer.

typeDefs.name: StackedRow
typeDefs.description: Row-oriented input to the `toStackedSeries(rows)` pivot helper.

fields.name: segments
fields.description: Per-layer values: { key, value, color? }. First non-undefined color per key wins.

typeDefs.name: StackedSegment
typeDefs.description: One layer of a stacked bar within a StackedRow.

fields.name: key
fields.description: Layer key — becomes the series id/label (e.g. "Done").

fields.name: color
fields.description: Optional CSS color for this layer.

fields.name: color
fields.description: Resolved mark color (the default tooltip tints its text with this).

fields.name: segments
fields.type: readonly { label: string; value: number; color?: string }[]
fields.description: Per-layer breakdown for a stacked category; the default stacked tooltip lists these.

examples.title: Basic bar chart
examples.code:
import { BarChart } from '@cascivo/charts'

const series = [{ id: 'a', label: 'Sales', data: [{x:'Jan',y:100},{x:'Feb',y:150}] }]
<BarChart series={series} x={d => d.x} y={d => d.y} title="Sales" />

examples.title: Stacked bar from row-oriented data

whenToUse: Comparing discrete categorical values across groups
Showing grouped or stacked multi-series data per category
whenNotToUse: Showing trends over continuous time — use LineChart
Part-of-whole proportions — use PieChart for ≤5 categories

related.name: LineChart
related.reason: Use for trends over continuous time

related.name: Histogram
related.reason: Use to show the distribution of a continuous variable

intent.a11yRationale: Renders with role="img" and requires a title prop for screen reader labeling.
